widget.spriteLabel: Tainá — click to chat, drag to move
widget.greeting: Hi, I'm Tainá! I can walk you through anything on GainForest.
widget.greetingHint: Pick a question below, or ask me anything.
widget.cantFind: I can't spot that on this page right now — but here's what to do:
widget.tourHint: I'll point at your screen. Click the highlighted spot to continue.
widget.signInFirst: You'll need to sign in for this one — use the Sign in button at the top of the page, then come back and I'll walk you through it.
widget.needProjectFirst: Donations are set up on a project, and you don't have one yet — create your first project, then come back here.
widget.createProjectFirst: Show me how to create a project

wallet.question: How do I receive donations?
wallet.title: Set up your organization's donation wallet
wallet.intro: Donations go to a shared wallet owned by your organization. The organization owner creates it in seconds with a passkey (Face ID or fingerprint) — no crypto experience needed.

open.title: Open your project
open.body: Go to My Projects and open the project you want to receive support for.

enable.title: Click “Enable Donations”
enable.body: On your project page you'll find the Support card on the right. Click Enable Donations.

addWallet.title: Create the organization wallet
addWallet.body: In the Donation Settings window, click Create wallet with passkey and confirm with Face ID, fingerprint, or a security key. Other members of the organization can add their own passkeys afterwards.

passkeys.title: Your wallet is ready
passkeys.body: Your project can accept donations as soon as the wallet exists. The wallet window shows the address and everyone who can approve payments. Other members of your organization can open it and add their own passkey too.

tour.open: Open the project you want to receive donations for.
tour.enable: Click Enable Donations in the Support card.
tour.addWallet: Click Create wallet with passkey and confirm with your passkey — that's it!

createProject.question: How do I set up a project?
createProject.title: Create your first project
createProject.intro: A project tells the story of your work and can receive support. The wizard takes about two minutes.

start.title: Start a new project
start.body: Click Create a Project in the sidebar, or Add project on your My Projects page.

name.title: Name it
name.body: Use a clear name people will understand, plus a one-line summary.

focus.title: Pick focus areas
focus.body: Choose the kinds of work that best describe the project — reforestation, monitoring, stewardship…

story.title: Add the timeline and story
story.body: Set a start date (ongoing is fine) and share what happened, how impact is measured, and who benefits.

people.title: Credit people and places
people.body: Add the people and groups behind the work, and attach the places where it happened.

photo.title: Add a photo
photo.body: A strong photo helps people recognise and trust your project.

review.title: Review and create
review.body: Check everything looks right, then click Create project. It's ready to share and receive support.

tour.start: Click Add project to open the wizard.
tour.wizard: Now follow the steps: name, focus areas, dates, story, people & places, photo — then Create project.

editProject.question: How do I edit a project?
editProject.title: Edit a project
editProject.intro: You can change everything about a project after publishing — name, story, photo, places.

myProjects.title: Go to My Projects
myProjects.body: Open your profile's Projects tab, or head to Manage from your profile menu.

edit.title: Click “Edit project”
edit.body: Every project card has an Edit project button.

save.title: Change and save
save.body: Update any field, change or remove the photo, then click Save changes at the bottom.

tour.edit: Click Edit project on the project you want to change.
tour.save: Make your changes, then hit Save changes at the bottom.

deleteProject.question: How do I delete a project?
deleteProject.title: Delete a project
deleteProject.intro: Deleting removes the project from your account. It cannot be undone — but your observations are never deleted with it.

editor.title: Open the project editor
editor.body: Go to My Projects and click Edit project — or open the project page and look for the Manage section.

delete.title: Click “Delete project”
delete.body: The delete button sits at the bottom of the editor.

confirm.title: Confirm by typing the name
confirm.body: Type the exact project name to confirm. This can't be undone.

tour.editor: First, open the editor of the project you want to remove.
tour.delete: Scroll down and click Delete project.
tour.confirm: Type the exact project name, then confirm. This can't be undone.

observations.question: How do I upload and manage observations?
observations.title: Upload and manage observations
observations.intro: Observations are photos of nature with a species, date, and place — the evidence behind your work.

add.title: Click “Add observations”
add.body: You'll find it in the sidebar and on your profile.

photos.title: Drop in your photos
photos.body: Upload up to 50 photos at once — AI reads each one and suggests what's in it.

check.title: Check and link
check.body: Confirm the species name, date, and location — and optionally link them to one of your projects. You can also hide your exact location.

publish.title: Add them
publish.body: Click Add and your sightings are published to your account.

manage.title: Manage them later
manage.body: Your profile's Observations tab lets you search, group observations into datasets, or select and delete them.

tour.photos: Drop your photos in — species suggestions run automatically. Check the names, link a project if you like, then click Add.
tour.manage: And this is where all your observations live: search them, group them into datasets, or select and delete.

bioblitz.question: How do I join the BioBlitz and win?
bioblitz.title: Join the BioBlitz challenge
bioblitz.intro: The BioBlitz is a friendly weekly race to record nature. Photograph any living thing, upload it, and climb the board.

open.title: Open BioBlitz
open.body: You'll find it in the sidebar under Funding.

register.title: Register to take part
register.body: One click — registering lets the team track your entries and send your prize if you win.

upload.title: Photograph and upload
upload.body: Head outside, photograph any living thing, and add it as observations during the round.

win.title: Win prizes
win.body: Most observations and best picture win cash prizes and a permanent profile badge — and every observation stays yours, reusable as evidence for your projects.

tour.register: Click Register to take part — one click and you're in.
tour.upload: Then upload nature photos here during the round — every observation counts.
